source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/010_Clinical Image Report  - A1417MCW.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for A1417MCW against HAD1417MCW using plain radiographic images acquired in DICOM 3.0.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by documenting comparative image quality, exposure conditions, image acquisition format, expert conclusion, and clinical evaluator qualifications.
keyPoints: Objective Device is A1417MCW by H&abyz and Comparative Equipment is HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
Output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used for downloading and analysis.
Thirty plain radiographic views were compared, with total average annotation values of 3.62 for HAD1417MCW and 3.63 for A1417MCW.
The conclusion states images obtained with A1417MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with HAD1417MCW.
The selected clinical evaluation expert is Professor Sung Hyun Kim, M.D., Ph.D., described as a board-certified radiologist in Korea with over 30 years of experience.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: FDA is referenced in expert qualification text, but the document itself does not provide an FDA submission identifier, FDA document date, or formal FDA status.
Product model identifier conflict: the document title and most tables use A1417MCW, but the Knee AP section lists F1417MCW.
Manufacturer spelling varies between H&abyz and H&Abyz in the source text.
Possible OCR or typographical issue: one row states DCIOM 3.0 instead of DICOM 3.0.
Document title is extracted from repeated page header text; the source path indicates Clinical Image Report, but the body title shown is Clinical Consideration 2.
